activation_mode.mechanism: Four activation modes documented: Always (alwaysApply: true injects rule into every prompt), Apply to Specific Files (globs frontmatter matches edited files via glob syntax), Apply Intelligently (description frontmatter lets the Agent determine relevance), and Apply Manually (no frontmatter triggers, referenced explicitly with @ruleName).

auto_memory.mechanism: Cursor does not document an AI-generated auto-memory system distinct from user-authored rules.

cross_provider_recognition.mechanism: Cursor documents AGENTS.md as an officially supported alternative to .cursor/rules/*.mdc — a plain markdown file without frontmatter metadata that is recognized and loaded alongside project rules.

file_imports.mechanism: MDC rule bodies may reference other files via @path syntax that inlines the referenced file into the rule context.

hierarchical_loading.mechanism: Rules can live in nested .cursor/rules/ directories within a project; rules in a subtree apply when working with files under that subtree, enabling monorepo-style scoping. AGENTS.md also supports nested files in subdirectories for granular context-specific guidance.

providerExtensions.name: MDC (Markdown + frontmatter) file format
providerExtensions.summary: Rules are .mdc or .md files — Markdown bodies with YAML-style frontmatter exposing description, alwaysApply, and globs fields. Both extensions are documented.

providerExtensions.name: Glob-based auto-attach
providerExtensions.summary: globs frontmatter array attaches a rule automatically whenever the agent edits or reads a matching file path (e.g., src/**/*.tsx, docs/**/*.md).

providerExtensions.name: alwaysApply frontmatter flag
providerExtensions.summary: alwaysApply: true injects the rule into every prompt regardless of file context; the primary Always activation mode mechanism.

providerExtensions.name: Agent-requested activation via description
providerExtensions.summary: A description-only frontmatter (no globs, no alwaysApply) lets the Agent decide when to pull the rule in based on the description text.

providerExtensions.name: Manual @ruleName reference
providerExtensions.summary: Rules without activation frontmatter can still be invoked explicitly by typing @<rule-name> in chat.

providerExtensions.name: @path file inlining inside rule bodies
providerExtensions.summary: @path tokens inside an .mdc body inline the referenced file's contents so rules can compose reusable snippets.

providerExtensions.name: Nested rule directories per subtree
providerExtensions.summary: Additional .cursor/rules/ directories in subfolders scope rules to that subtree — supports monorepo layouts where each package has its own rules.

providerExtensions.name: Legacy .cursorrules file
providerExtensions.summary: A single .cursorrules file at the project root is still honored for backwards compatibility; the .cursor/rules/ directory is the current recommended layout.

providerExtensions.name: AGENTS.md plain markdown alternative
providerExtensions.summary: Cursor officially recognizes AGENTS.md as a simpler markdown alternative to .cursor/rules/*.mdc — no frontmatter metadata required; nested AGENTS.md files in subdirectories provide granular context-specific guidance.

providerExtensions.name: Team rules via dashboard
providerExtensions.summary: Enterprise plan users can enforce organization-wide rules through the Cursor dashboard with enforcement options; rules are distributed to all team members without requiring per-repo file changes.

providerExtensions.name: Remote rules from GitHub repositories
providerExtensions.summary: Rules can be imported from external GitHub repositories directly into a project, enabling shared rule sets across teams without copying files.

providerExtensions.name: /create-rule slash command
providerExtensions.summary: Typing /create-rule in chat scaffolds a new rule file; Cursor also surfaces rule creation through the settings UI.
